title: Noctua NH-U14S DX4677 Quick Look With the Intel Xeon 600 Series
gist: STH unboxes the Noctua NH-U14S DX4677 heatsink, a variant of Noctua's established NH-U14S platform fitted with a larger contact plate to cool the LGA4710-socketed Intel Xeon 6 series (excluding the 6900E+/6900P), purchased to cool a 250W Intel Xeon 658X sample.

insights.claim: The Noctua NH-U14S DX4677 heatsink is compatible with Intel's LGA4710 socket, including the 250W Intel Xeon 658X processor STH needed to cool, provided the correct carrier frame is used.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Confirms a specific, verified cooling solution for high-TDP (250W) Intel Xeon W parts, useful for workstation/server builders sourcing compatible coolers.
insights.locator: Recently, we needed a heatsink for the Intel Xeon 658X

insights.claim: The NH-U14S DX4677 is Noctua's existing, well-regarded NH-U14S heatsink/fan platform fitted with a larger contact plate to support Intel Xeon Sapphire Rapids/Emerald Rapids and the Xeon 6 series, but only the smaller-socket Xeon 6 models, explicitly excluding the Xeon 6900E+ and 6900P series.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Buyers need to check socket variant carefully: this cooler does not cover Intel's largest Xeon 6900E+/6900P sockets, a compatibility gap relevant to datacenter/workstation cooling procurement.
insights.locator: The naming on this one makes a lot of sense

insights.claim: The NH-U14S DX4677 ships with pre-applied thermal interface material, a feature STH says it prefers on server and workstation cooling units, consistent with the preference noted in its prior Arctic Freezer 4U-SP5 Mini review.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Pre-applied TIM is emerging as an expected standard feature among server/workstation cooler vendors, a minor but recurring quality signal STH uses across coolers it reviews.
insights.quote: We noted in our Arctic Freezer 4U-SP5 Mini Review how we prefer pre-applied thermal interface material on the server and workstation units. Noctua did that here.
insights.locator: Moving to the bottom is something very good

insights.claim: The cooler uses two Noctua NF-A15 140mm PWM fans that keep it quiet but give the unit a tall profile, and it ships with an installation tool and Y-cable, features the author notes are handy for labs that swap CPUs frequently.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Minor usability signal for lab/test environments that repeatedly swap CPUs, less relevant to one-time deployments.
insights.quote: That is always handy, especially if you do not have a lab like at STH, where you are swapping out CPUs weekly.
insights.locator: Noctua also provides a Y cable and a tool
